notes: USB drag-and-drop flashing, extremely easy to get started. No WiFi/BLE, choose Pico W if you need networking

onboard_peripherals.name: LED
onboard_peripherals.notes: Onboard indicator LED. Most projects don't need to control it, GPIO25 can be freed

notes: RP2040 peripherals are fixed to GPIO pins. Each function can only be selected from the set of pins listed in pin_options
I2C0/I2C1: 6 choose 1, each pair (sda and scl must be from the same group)
SPI0/SPI1: 4-6 choose 1, each pin comes from a fixed GPIO group
PWM: Every GPIO can do PWM (8 slices total, 2 channels per slice)
ADC: Only GPIO 26/27/28 can be used, GPIO 29 is ADC reference or input (varies by board)
GPIO 23 is SMPS control, generally not used
GPIO 24 is VBUS detection, generally not used
GPIO 25 is the onboard LED, usable but recommended not to repurpose
Default I2C0 (GPIO4/5) is the most common, be careful not to conflict with UART1 (default TX=4/RX=5)
